posts.name: Doug Lee
posts.cooked:
• 2002 Domaine Chandon de Briailles Corton-Clos du Roi - France, Burgundy, Côte de Beaune, Corton Grand Cru (10/29/2016)
    Medium dark ruby color. Brisk nose of pine forest, anise, dark berry, alpine floral notes, stone. Fresh and surprisingly lithe on the palate with layers of mulberry, red and black raspberry, holiday spice, stone, and iron integrating nicely. Bright acidity and firm but managed tannin leading to a longer finish of stony red fruit laced with tart grip. Great with simple roast chicken. This wine is very accessible in its adolescence and has lots of upside. (92 pts.)

Posted from CellarTracker

posts.name: Doug Lee
posts.cooked:
Scott and Alan,

Yes, I’m becoming a big fan too, although my experience is limited. Pure wines.

Cheers,
Doug

posts.name: Alan Rath
posts.cooked: It is probably Chandon’s best red wine. The 2010 model was fabulous on release, can’t wait to see how it turns out in a decade or so.

posts.name: alan weinberg
posts.cooked: Corton takes forever but can be worth the wait.

posts.name: Howard Cooper
posts.cooked:
Doug Lee:
  • 2002 Domaine Chandon de Briailles Corton-Clos du Roi - France, Burgundy, Côte de Beaune, Corton Grand Cru (10/29/2016)
    Medium dark ruby color. Brisk nose of pine forest, anise, dark berry, alpine floral notes, stone. Fresh and surprisingly lithe on the palate with layers of mulberry, red and black raspberry, holiday spice, stone, and iron integrating nicely. Bright acidity and firm but managed tannin leading to a longer finish of stony red fruit laced with tart grip. Great with simple roast chicken. This wine is very accessible in its adolescence and has lots of upside. (92 pts.)

Posted from CellarTracker

I do not have this wine, but I do have 2002 Corton Bressandes from them. You describe the wine as having plenty of upside. When would you drink this? Thanks.

Another fan of Chandon de Briailles.

posts.name: Doug Lee
posts.cooked:
Alan and Alan

This one was youthful and flavorful. Not many nooks and crannies yet but very enjoyable.

Cheers,
Doug

posts.name: Doug Lee
posts.cooked:
Howard,

Could drink now but I suspect it will evolve for 5-8 years. The vintage is part of the equation; others are more expert but 02 seems more vivacious but perhaps less disciplined than other heralded vintages IMHO.

Cheers,
Doug

posts.name: Andy B
posts.cooked:
Doug Lee:
  • 2002 Domaine Chandon de Briailles Corton-Clos du Roi - France, Burgundy, Côte de Beaune, Corton Grand Cru (10/29/2016)
    Medium dark ruby color. Brisk nose of pine forest, anise, dark berry, alpine floral notes, stone. Fresh and surprisingly lithe on the palate with layers of mulberry, red and black raspberry, holiday spice, stone, and iron integrating nicely. Bright acidity and firm but managed tannin leading to a longer finish of stony red fruit laced with tart grip. Great with simple roast chicken. This wine is very accessible in its adolescence and has lots of upside. (92 pts.)

Posted from CellarTracker

Nice note, Doug. I had a bottle of this 3-4 weeks ago and had a very similar experience. This wine does drink well now with air, and should develop further with time. Opening a full case to serve next Saturday–hoping not too much bottle variation!

posts.name: Doug Lee
posts.cooked:
Andy,

Wow, that sounds like an occasion. Would be curious about bottle consistency also. Hope they all show well.

Cheers,
Doug

posts.name: Howard Cooper
posts.cooked:
Doug Lee:

Howard,

Could drink now but I suspect it will evolve for 5-8 years. The vintage is part of the equation; others are more expert but 02 seems more vivacious but perhaps less disciplined than other heralded vintages IMHO.

Cheers,
Doug

thanks Doug. My sense is that a lot of 2002s are starting to drink well now, including their PV les Vergelesses. But, Cortons take a long time so I was not sure about this one.

posts.name: William Kelley
posts.cooked: Tasted the 2015 rendition of this, along with all the other wines in the cellar, earlier this month. It’s absolutely fantastic, as are all the reds. Place your orders now and thank me later.

posts.name: George Chadwick
posts.cooked: Tasted a mini vertical of their Bressandes. Very pure winemaking, very vintage sensitive in a good way, I would not use one vintage to guess whether others are ready. I was really gratified and impressed. Femininity in a Corton.

posts.name: Doug Lee
posts.cooked:
William,

Good to know. Will look forward to their release.


George,

I will be on the lookout for the Bressandes. Nice descriptor.



Cheers,
Doug

posts.name: Andy B
posts.cooked: The CdB Marechaudes is no slouch, either. A bottle of the '95 was hauntingly good (and not on Halloween) and outpaced the '95 Bressandes poured side by side a few years ago.
